title: Bridge Essay: Different brain structures related to self- and external-agency attribution: a b

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nested Agency** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nested agency — voluntary action, free will as constructed experience, intentional binding.

**Different brain structures related to self- and external-agency attribution: abrief review and meta-analysis.**. Brain Struct Funct.. PMID: 21212978. DOI: 10.1007/s00429-010-0298-1. Several neuroimaging studies have consistently shown activations of areas surrounding the temporo-parietal junction (TPJ) during tasks exploring the sense of agency. Beyond TPJ, activations in different structures, such as the dorsolateral prefrontal cortex (dLPFC), the pre-supplementary motor area (pre-SMA), the insula and the precuneus have been reported.
